summary: MSAL authority reverted from '/organizations' to '/common' (personal MS account support). PRIMARY ASSERTION PASSED: clicking [data-testid=auth-microsoft-btn] on /login now navigates to 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=5de6c39a-bf40-4f42-88d8-f755a7797cc9&scope=openid%20email%20profile%20offline_access&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fiema-ai-platform.preview.emergentagent.com&...&response_type=code&code_challenge_method=S256' — URL contains '/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize' and does NOT contain '/organizations/'. state param base64-decodes to include interactionType:'redirect' confirming loginRedirect (not popup). Verified via static grep that all 3 target files use '/common': AuthPage.jsx L158, MsalRedirectHandler.jsx L27, mobile SocialAuthButtons.js L24-25. Regressions all green: admin login (siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com) → /chat; fresh register (TEST_iter7_ui_<ts>@example.com) → /chat with toast 'Account created — enjoy your 100 welcome credits!'; Facebook button fires info toast; Google GSI iframe renders; Apple SDK loaded; MsalRedirectHandler silent on '/'; zero pageerrors. Backend pytest 7/7 pass in 3.98s: /auth/oauth-config returns google/microsoft/apple enabled+facebook disabled with all client_ids present; /auth/microsoft-verify, /auth/apple, /auth/google-verify all return 400 for junk tokens; register+immediate-login same-second returns 200+200 with distinct access & refresh tokens (jti uniqueness); back-to-back logins same-second distinct tokens; refresh flow distinct tokens.
action_items: USER ACTION (external, cannot be tested here): In Azure Portal for app client_id=5de6c39a-bf40-4f42-88d8-f755a7797cc9, set 'Supported account types' to 'Accounts in any organizational directory AND personal Microsoft accounts' — required for the personal account siddharthbose.dev@gmail.com to complete sign-in against the /common authority. Without this Azure change, Microsoft will still reject personal accounts even though the frontend now sends /common.
USER ACTION (unchanged): The end-to-end MS OAuth flow (with real 2FA) cannot be validated headlessly. User must smoke-test manually after applying the Azure 'Supported account types' change.
critical_code_review_comments: SocialAuthButtons.js (mobile) still hardcodes GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID/APPLE_SERVICE_ID at the top — should move to expo-constants extra for production builds. Not blocking.

MsalRedirectHandler.jsx creates its own PublicClientApplication instance (not shared with AuthPage's module-level cache). If a user lands on /login post-redirect, both handlers may race on handleRedirectPromise(). The sessionStorage.iema_msal_pending marker mitigates double-processing, but consider consolidating to a single handler for cleanliness.
AuthPage.jsx L158 comment now accurately documents the /common requirement — good.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (primary MS-redirect URL check + admin login + fresh register + Facebook toast + Google GSI + Apple SDK + Landing silent-redirect)

test_credentials: Admin siddharth.bose@iemlabs.com / Admin@12345; dynamic TEST_iter7_ui_<ts>@example.com / Test@12345 for UI; TEST_iter5_<uuid>@example.com for pytest
seed_data_creation: 1 fresh UI registration + 3 pytest registrations per run. Negligible volume, not cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MSAL authority is now '/common' in all 3 places (AuthPage.jsx L158, MsalRedirectHandler.jsx L27, mobile SocialAuthButtons.js L24-25). Captured MS auth URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=5de6c39a-bf40-4f42-88d8-f755a7797cc9&... — confirmed '/common/' present, '/organizations/' absent. All 7 backend pytest tests pass in test_iter5_verify.py. Facebook toast + Google GSI iframe + Apple SDK all healthy. Admin login and fresh register both reach /chat. Full end-to-end MS OAuth (with real credentials + 2FA) is out of headless scope — user must complete the Azure 'Supported account types' change and smoke-test manually.
rca of the issue: Bug: After user completed Microsoft 2FA with personal MS account (siddharthbose.dev@gmail.com — Gmail-based consumer MS account), Microsoft looped back to sign-in page without surfacing an error, because MSAL authority was set to '/organizations' which explicitly rejects personal Microsoft accounts. Fix: reverted authority to '/common' in AuthPage.jsx (getMsal instance) + MsalRedirectHandler.jsx (fallback instance) + mobile SocialAuthButtons.js (MS_DISCOVERY endpoints for both authorize & token). Verification: primary URL-substring assertion on MS button click confirms '/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize' is now in the outbound URL (not '/organizations/'). End-to-end validation requires the corresponding Azure Portal change (Supported account types → 'any org + personal MS accounts') which is external to this codebase and must be applied and smoke-tested manually by the user.
